rulings.Case Title: CONTRACTORS ACCESS PROGRAM OF CALIFORNIA, A VS. MAJESTIC CAPITAL, LTD., A FOREIGN CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Otion To Compel Production Of Documents By Two Bridges Ltd
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, February 4, 2013, at 9:00 am, line 1, PLAINTIFF CONTRACTORS ACCESS PROGRAM OF CALIFORNIA'S, Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Production Of Documents By Two Bridges Ltd Pro Tem Judge Katherine Huibonhoa, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem has not issued a tentative ruling on this motion. Each party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing. =302/JPT

rulings.Case Title: CREDITORS TRADE ASSOCIATION, INC. DBA VS. SUEDE BLUE, INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Award Of Attorney'S Fees
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, February 4, 2013, Line 20, PLAINTIFF CREDITORS TRADE ASSOCIATION, INC.'s Motion For Award Of Attorney'S Fees. The motion is granted to the extent that the Court awards a total of $1500 as reasonable fees. The fees were reduced because counsel's declaration states that he prepared "plaintiff's writ of attachment, draft memorandum of points and authorities, draft declarations, finalize, file and serve," (para. 9) and that he "prepared default judgment papers, including interest declaration, cost declaration, supporting affidavit in lieu of testimony; assembly of exhibits; preparation of Doe dismissal; judgment and other documents necessary to comply with Local Rules to establish the right to a default judgment; preparation and service of case management statement"(para 10). However, the Court's register of actions do not reflect these events occurred in this case. Further, $640 in discovery sanctions are already reflected in the Stipulated Judgment filed October 22, 2012, yet $600 in attorney fees are requested on the invoice submitted in connection with this motion. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: RASHID ALATRAQCHI VS. METRO CAB LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Omtion To Getting Like His Waybill He Employed By Defts
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, February 4, 2013, at 9:00 am, line 2, PLAINTIFF RASHID ALATRAQCHI'S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Getting Like His Waybill He Employed By Defendants Pro Tem Judge Katherine Huibonhoa, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion is denied, for all of the reasons set forth in the opposition papers. Specifically, the Motion is the same substantive motion already decided by Order of the Court on October 10, 2012, which has not been directly challenged. The Motion is also denied on the grounds that (1) plaintiff failed to meet and confer; (2) it appears to be a discovery motion but does not comply with the requirements of the California Civil Discovery Act; and (3) the motion is without any predicate because Plaintiff has not properly requested through discovery the items he now seeks to compel. Sanctions are denied, but the Court notes that this is the second time Plaintiff appears to have brought this unsubstantiated motion, which was previously denied by the Court. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to katherinehuibonhoa@paulhastings.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/JPT
